name: Satoru Gojo and Suguru Geto
description:

More Info

What is Polyamory?

Polyamory is the ability (and choice) to love more than one person at the same time — romantically and/or sexually — with the full knowledge and consent of everyone involved. It's about open, honest relationships with emotional depth.

Core Principles

Consent: Everyone involved agrees to the relationship structure.

Transparency: Feelings, boundaries, and changes are discussed openly.

Responsibility: Each relationship is treated with care and respect.

No secrecy: Honesty is at the center, even when conversations are hard.

Common Relationship Structures

V-Relationship: One person is romantically involved with two others, who are not involved with each other.

Triad: All three partners are romantically involved with one another.

Relationship Network: Multiple people connected in various ways and relationship types.

Hierarchical: One “primary” partner, with others considered “secondary.”

Non-hierarchical: All relationships are treated equally, without rankings.

Frequent rules in Polyamorous relationships

Honest Communication: All feelings, insecurities, or concerns should be openly discussed — even when it’s uncomfortable.

No Secret Meetups: Intimacy or dates with other partners are shared openly and not hidden.

Safe Sex Practices: Health and safety are top priorities — regular testing, clear agreements.

Respecting Emotional Boundaries: No one is forced into emotional or physical closeness they don’t want.

Time Management: Time and attention are shared fairly — often with open scheduling or planning.

No Comparisons or Competitiveness: Each relationship is unique —comparison leads to resentment.
